summary: DeepGuard AI backend fully tested via pytest (17/17 passed). Health, Emergent Google Auth (401 paths, seeded mongo session -> /auth/me works, logout invalidates token, all protected endpoints reject anon requests). Detection endpoints /detect/frame (persist=false and persist=true), /detect/video (3 real face frames), and /detect/audio (real waveform PNG) all invoke Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY and return valid JSON with fake_probability, verdict, faces_detected, artifacts and non-empty reasoning (verified end-to-end, not just 200 shapes). Verified persistence: /detections list excludes thumbnails, /detections/{id} returns full doc with thumbnail, /analytics/summary aggregates counts+by_mode+by_verdict+recent correctly. Real sample face JPEG downloaded from Pexels (640x360) and saved at /app/test_fixtures/face.jpg; waveform PNG generated at /app/test_fixtures/waveform.png.

minor.issue: Uses raw dict indexing (data['email'], data['session_token']) on Emergent oauth response - if provider returns malformed JSON this will 500 instead of 401. Wrap in try/except and return 401.
FastAPI on_event is deprecated; migrate to lifespan handlers to avoid future breakage.

Thumbnail stored as raw base64 slice [:200000]; a random cut of base64 can be un-decodable in the UI. Consider decoding+resizing to a small JPEG or saving the first N *bytes decoded* instead of first 200k of base64 chars.
frames_base64 capped at 6 silently; consider surfacing this in response metadata for the UI.

action_items: None critical. Optional hardening: (1) harden /auth/session response parsing, (2) migrate on_event -> lifespan, (3) reconsider thumbnail base64-slice strategy.
critical_code_review_comments: get_current_user correctly excludes Mongo _id via projections and handles cookie OR Bearer token uniformly - good.
Detection endpoints correctly separate no-persist vs persist paths and always compute latency_ms server-side - good.
/analytics/summary uses aggregations plus count_documents; efficient for expected volume.
server.py is ~380 lines - still manageable but if adding real-time / websocket streaming it should be split into routers (auth, detection, history).
process_session assumes Emergent OAuth response contains 'email' and 'session_token' unconditionally - add validation.
Detection thumbnail slicing may produce invalid base64 (see minor issue).
Deprecated FastAPI on_event handler (see minor issue).
test_credentials: Seeded per-test-session in Mongo test_database.users + user_sessions via pymongo fixture (user_id: user_test_<uuid>, session_token: sess_test_<uuid>, expires_at: +7d). Cleaned up after session.
seed_data_creation: backend_test.py session fixture seeds one TEST_ user + session_token, and creates 3 detection records (webcam/video/audio) via the real detection endpoints; teardown removes users, user_sessions, and detections for that test user_id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. Test fixtures at /app/test_fixtures/face.jpg (Pexels 640x360 JPEG) and /app/test_fixtures/waveform.png. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + /api. To auth, seed test_database.users + user_sessions directly (see auth_testing.md and the seeded_session fixture in backend_test.py). Detection endpoints call Claude Sonnet 4.5 - each call ~5-15s. Total real Claude calls per full run = 4 (frame no-persist, frame persist, video, audio). Suite runs in ~35s with -n 2 loadscope. Note: TestDetection and TestHistory must remain in one class for xdist loadscope to share pytest.detection_id_frame across tests.
rca of the issue: N/A - no product-side bugs. Initial 3 test failures were purely test-infra (xdist -n 2 loadscope split TestDetection and TestHistory onto different workers, so pytest.detection_id_frame set in worker gw1 was invisible in gw0). Fixed by merging history tests into TestDetection class, which loadscope pins to a single worker.
